QF.id: Introduction to AML, CFT and Proliferation Financing_26

question.text: Which international body was established in 1989 specifically to set standards and promote effective implementation of measures for combating money laundering?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** FATF was established in 1989 by the G-7 Summit in Paris specifically to examine and develop measures to combat money laundering. It sets international standards (the FATF Recommendations) that countries are expected to implement. **The Trap:** The IMF and World Bank deal with broader financial stability, not specifically AML standards. The Basel Committee focuses on banking supervision standards. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series IX (Merchant Banking) also references FATF as the global AML standard-setter. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Understanding FATF's role helps you appreciate why Indian AML regulations follow certain international patterns — India is a FATF member and must comply with its recommendations.
